Ad Text:
About the job




RGP is a global consulting firm helping some of the most recognized companies in the world work differently. Our success comes from a shared belief in rolling up our sleeves and doing the hard work of solving complex challenges, executing plans and implementing technology to help transform organizations. Our Australia team supports global and local clients to transform their business, optimise their processes and design scalable business models with an agile and client centred approach. With Global Supply Chains increasingly under pressure, we are working with a number of global clients to transform their SCM approaches and restructure their operating model with strategic advice and hands-on implementation of Supply Chain Optimisation projects. RGP is seeking to add to our bench of experienced, consultative and hands-on Supply Chain projects talent in Sydney and Melbourne.




Working as a consultant, you will be connected to work that matters, putting your expertise to its best use while developing skills for the future. The result is a career defined by you, supported by RGP and built on tangible accomplishments.

As we continue to grow and deepen our impact on organizations around the world, we are looking for the right people to join us on our mission. If you are interested in being part of our team, there a few things you should know:

· We are energized by challenges and the effort needed to solve them.

· We like working with people who are positive, adaptable and growth-minded.

· We care how work gets accomplished and are deeply invested in the success of our clients and our colleagues.




If this sounds like you, we invite you to read on and learn more.




THE RGP CONSULTING EXPERIENCE

RGP has a unique consulting model that provides opportunities for consultants to work in different ways. Most consultants work in a model where they have the flexibility and autonomy to choose projects aligning to their experience, skills and desired lifestyle. Other opportunities exist in team-based, strategy and solutions work. In all models, you will feel the full support of RGP to assist you in your work and guide you on your chosen path.




PURPOSE OF THE ROLE

We are seeking a hands-on Process Documentation Business Analyst to support a project-based initiative focused on transforming supply chain operations and improving business processes.

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys getting into the detail, documenting processes, and working closely with business users.




Key Responsibilities

Gather requirements through workshops and stakeholder discussions

Document as-is and to-be processes across supply chain, finance, and systems

Map workflows for warehouse, distribution, and 3PL operations

Support system-related process changes (ERP, CRM, planning tools)

Assist with implementation support and process updates

Work in an agile environment with changing priorities




Qualifications

3–6 years’ experience as a Business Analyst or Process Analyst

Strong experience in process mapping and documentation

Exposure to supply chain, logistics, or distribution processes

Experience with SAP (S/4HANA preferred) or similar ERP systems

Familiarity with integrated systems (planning, CRM, SFDC)

Comfortable working in fast-moving project environments

Available immediately to 4 weeks

Job Title: Business Analyst – Systems Assessment - Fed Government | National Opportunity
Ad Text:
NV1 Eligible | 12 Month Contract | Hybrid Working

About the Role

We are seeking an experienced Business Analyst to support a Federal Government engagement focused on the assessment of ICT systems, operational processes, and support arrangements within a complex, multi-stakeholder environment.

This role is suited to a Business Analyst who can quickly understand requirements, engage effectively with stakeholders, and deliver clear, structured outputs to support decision-making.

Key Responsibilities

Analyse and document current state systems, processes, and support arrangements

Engage with technical and non-technical stakeholders

Capture business processes and system requirements

Identify gaps and improvement opportunities

Produce structured reports and recommendations

Skills and Experience

Experience in system or operational assessments (discovery-focused roles)

Strong stakeholder engagement across technical and business teams

Experience documenting business processes, ICT system requirements, and support models

Ability to produce clear, decision-ready outputs

Experience in Federal Government or similar environments

What Will Make You Competitive

Proven ability to deliver outcomes in complex environments

Strong written communication and structured thinking

Ability to provide practical, fit-for-purpose recommendations

Location and Working Arrangements

This role is open to candidates across Australia (QLD, WA, ACT, VIC, NSW, NT, SA, TAS) under a hybrid working model.

Candidates based in major metropolitan areas (Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane, Canberra, Perth) are particularly encouraged to apply.

Security Clearance

Applicants must be Australian Citizens and able to obtain a Negative Vetting Level 1 (NV1) security clearance. Candidates who already hold an active NV1 clearance are strongly encouraged to apply.

How to Apply

If you are a Business Analyst with experience in systems assessment, stakeholder engagement, and structured analysis, we encourage you to apply.

Applications without responses to the screening questions may not be considered.

National Scope - Supporting Glencore Metals Australia
Brisbane, Queensland, Australia
Lead positive change

Senior - HRIS Analyst – Workday (Internal Vacancy Only)

Zinc Australia Human Resources

Brisbane, Queensland, Australia

Ref. No. 100434

Role Purpose

Three HRIS systems are being consolidated into one system (Workday). HCM implementation is commencing, led by the global team, and will be implemented by January 2027. This role is an opportunity to be part of the system design and implementation, with long term substantive role as Senior – Workday Analyst.

The successful candidate will be the Australian subject matter authority for Workday across Glencore Metals Australia (GMA).

This role owns the integrity, optimisation and governance of Workday within Australia, ensuring the system enables operational efficiency, compliance and strategic workforce insight across geographically dispersed mining and processing assets.

Operating within global Workday governance frameworks, this position translates Australian operational requirements into scalable, compliant system solutions and acts as the primary escalation point for complex system issues.

This position is based residentially in Brisbane, requiring a minimum of four (4) days in office and one (1) day remote allowed per week.

Scope & Impact

National remit across Australian Metals Assets
Tier 2/3 escalation authority within GMA
Accountable for Australian Workday configuration integrity
Influences HR, Payroll, IT and operational stakeholders
Critical to post-implementation stabilisation and optimisation

This role directly impacts payroll accuracy, workforce compliance, audit outcomes and executive workforce reporting.

Key Accountabilities

1. Workday Superuser Authority

Act as the Australian escalation point for complex Workday issues.
Own local configuration integrity within global design constraints.
Assess and coordinate impact of biannual Workday releases.
Lead UAT cycles for Australian changes.
Maintain system documentation, change logs and governance controls
2. System Optimisation & Continuous Improvement

Identify and deliver enhancements that improve HR operational efficiency.
Reduce manual interventions and process workarounds.
Optimise business process frameworks aligned to Australian industrial environments.
Drive digital adoption across HR
3. Governance, Compliance & Risk Control

Ensure system alignment with Australian employment legislation and enterprise agreements.
Partner with Payroll to maintain data accuracy and system control integrity.
Support audit, compliance and regulatory reporting requirements.
Monitor data quality and proactively mitigate risk exposures.
4. Reporting & Workforce Insight

Develop executive dashboards and operational reporting.
Translate workforce data into actionable insights for HR and business leaders.
Ensure integrity of headcount, labour cost and workforce metrics.
 5. Stakeholder Partnership

Act as primary Australian interface with Global Workday governance.
Provide training and capability uplift to HR users.
Partner with:

o             HR Business Partners

o             Payroll & Finance

o             IT / Digital teams

o             Asset HR leads 

You will have the following skills and experience:

Technical 

Advanced Workday HCM expertise (5+ years).
Strong knowledge of security frameworks and business process configuration.
Experience within Australian payroll and industrial relations environments.
Advanced Workday reporting and calculated field capability.
Experience supporting integrations and system testing cycles.

Commercial & Behavioural 

Strong risk awareness and governance mindset.
High attention to detail and data integrity.
Able to operate autonomously within a global framework.
Confident engaging with senior stakeholders.
Continuous improvement orientation.
Qualifications

Degree in HR, Information Systems, Business or related field.
Workday certifications will be highly regarded.

Applications close at 5pm on Friday 27 March 2026.

We are partnering with a globally recognised real‑assets institution to support the continued expansion of their Infrastructure and Renewables coverage. The team operates with the focus and agility of a boutique, supported by the scale, research capability and deal flow of a bulge bracket IB. Strong momentum in the market is the driving force behind the additional hires, with multiple live mandates across renewables, project finance and M&A, and a growing APAC pipeline supported by the broader regional business.

‍

You’ll work closely with the Head of Infrastructure across client management, transaction execution and the ongoing growth of the platform whilst mentoring and supporting the development of junior team members. The role offers exposure to M&A, project finance and equity capital raises, primarily across renewable and energy transition assets.

‍

While strong modelling literacy is required, Senior Analysts cover the heavy technical aspects and model building duties. The ideal profile will have:

6+ years experience within M&A and/or Project Finance experience within infrastructure or renewables
Strong exposure to renewable energy transactions
A commercial, client‑facing mindset
Experience mentoring junior bankers

‍

This is a rare opportunity to step into a senior seat with real influence over strategy, execution and long‑term trajectory to D/MD levels in the coming years.
